title: 10 best herbal creams for numbness in hands

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with numbness in hands because of its unique properties. The creams contain Ginkgo biloba extract, which has been shown to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation. This increased blood flow helps to restore feeling and sensation to the affected areas, reducing numbness and tingling. Additionally, the antioxidants present in Maidenhair tree creams also help to protect against damage caused by free radicals, promoting overall health and well-being of the hands.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Ginkgo biloba extracts have been shown to reduce inflammation and pain, which could potentially help alleviate numbness in hands.
Improved blood flow: The antioxidants and flavonoids present in Ginkgo biloba extracts can help improve blood circulation, which may aid in reducing numbness caused by poor circulation.
Neuroprotective properties: Some studies suggest that Ginkgo biloba extract may have neuroprotective effects, which could potentially help reduce nerve damage or compression-related numbness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the maidenhair tree contain compounds that help to stimulate blood flow and relieve numbness and tingling sensations in the hands.
Barks: The bark of the maidenhair tree is rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in reducing swelling and numbness in the hands.
remedy_recipe: Grind 250mg of dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered ginkgo biloba with 20g of coconut oil, 10g of shea butter, and 5g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until all ingredients are fully incorporated and melted.
Remove the double boiler from heat and let it cool to around 60°c while stirring occasionally.
Pour the cooled mixture into small jars and store them in the refrigerator at a temperature below 25°c for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bell pepper creams helps with numbness in hands because they contain a unique blend of herbs and antioxidants that work together to stimulate blood flow and reduce inflammation. The capsaicin present in bell peppers helps to increase circulation, allowing oxygen and nutrients to reach the affected areas, thereby alleviating numbness and tingling sensations in the hands. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of the herbs help to reduce swelling and discomfort associated with numbness, providing relief and promoting overall hand health.
remedy_properties: Capsaicin: Found in chili peppers, capsaicin has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate numbness or tingling sensations in the hands by reducing inflammation and blocking pain receptors.

Ginger: Ginger contains compounds like gingerol and shogaol that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce numbness and tingling sensations in the hands by improving blood circulation and reducing inflammation.

Arnica: Arnica gel or cream, derived from the arnica plant, has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that may help alleviate numbness or tingling sensations in the hands by reducing swelling and pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Bell pepper roots contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help relieve numbness and tingling sensations in the hands.
Seeds: Bell pepper seeds are rich in omega-3 fatty acids and antioxidants, which can help reduce inflammation and improve circulation to alleviate numbness in the hands.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of fresh capsicum annuum peppers and wash them thoroughly under running water.
Dry the capsicum annuum peppers for 24 hours or until they are completely dry and brittle.
Grind 10 grams of dried capsicum annuum peppers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the powdered capsicum annuum with 50 milliliters of coconut oil and let it steep for 2 weeks in an airtight container.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a nut milk bag and discard the solids to obtain the herbal cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mountain arnica creams helps with numbness in hands because they contain natural anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm irritated nerves. The cream's ability to penetrate deep into the skin allows it to target the root cause of numbness, which is often inflammation or pressure on nerve endings. By reducing inflammation and promoting healthy blood flow, mountain arnica creams can help restore sensation and feeling in affected areas, providing relief from tingling, numbness, and pins-and-needles sensations.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Arnica's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as helenin and flavonoids, help reduce swelling and inflammation in the affected areas, which can alleviate numbness and tingling sensations.

Pain-relieving properties: Arnica contains sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to exhibit analgesic and antispasmodic effects, helping to relieve pain and discomfort associated with numbness or paresthesia in the hands.

Vaso-dilatory properties: The vasodilating effects of arnica's beta-carotene and other compounds can help increase blood flow to the affected areas, which may improve circulation and alleviate numbness and tingling sensations caused by poor circulation or nerve compression.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the mountain arnica plant are rich in sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help reduce numbness and pain in hands.
Buds: Mountain arnica buds contain a high concentration of helenin, a compound that has been shown to have a profound impact on reducing inflammation and numbness in the hands.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh arnica montana flowers and dry them completely for two weeks in a cool dark place.
Grind the dried arnica montana flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix one part of the powdered arnica montana with three parts of organic coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat.
Stir the mixture for 30 minutes to combine thoroughly, then remove from heat and let it cool down to room temperature.
Pour the cooled mixture into glass jars and store them in a cool dark place for at least four weeks before using.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle creams helps with numbness in hands because of its ability to improve circulation and reduce inflammation. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties help to calm down irritated nerves, reducing numbness and tingling sensations. Additionally, stinging nettle's natural vasodilatory effects increase blood flow to the affected area, allowing oxygen and nutrients to reach the nerve endings more effectively. This improved circulation helps to stimulate nerve function, alleviating numbness and restoring sensation in the hands.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Stinging nettle cream reduces inflammation, which can alleviate numbness caused by compression or irritation of nerves, allowing for improved blood flow and sensation.
Pain-relieving property: Nettle's analgesic properties help to reduce pain and discomfort associated with numbness, making it easier to perform daily activities without discomfort.

Vasodilatory property: Stinging nettle's ability to dilate blood vessels improves circulation, which can increase oxygenation of the hands and fingers, helping to reduce numbness and tingling sensations.
remedy_parts: Roots: Stinging nettle roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and numbness in the hands.
Barks: The barks of the stinging nettle plant have analgesic properties, making them effective in reducing pain and numbness associated with hand conditions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh nettle leaves in early morning dew for their highest potency and quality.
Dry the harvested nettle leaves using a food dehydrator at 35°c for 24 hours.
Blend 30g of dried nettle leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar pestle.
Combine the powdered nettle with 100ml of sweet almond oil and 20ml of beeswax in a heatproof bowl.
Melt the mixture in a double boiler for 10 minutes, then stir until smooth and consistent.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Frankincense creams helps with numbness in hands because they contain boswellic acids, which have potent anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically, these acids help reduce swelling and inflammation in the hands, allowing for improved blood flow and nerve conduction. This increased circulation and reduced inflammation can alleviate numbness caused by conditions like carpal tunnel syndrome, arthritis, or repetitive strain injuries. Additionally, frankincense's natural analgesic properties can also provide relief from pain associated with these conditions, promoting a sense of comfort and relaxation in the affected hands.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Frankincense creams contain boswellic acids, which reduce inflammation and swelling in the body, helping to alleviate numbness in hands caused by conditions like carpal tunnel syndrome.
Antioxidant properties: Frankincense contains antioxidants that help protect against oxidative stress and damage to tissues, which can contribute to nerve damage and numbness in hands.

Pain-relieving and analgesic effects: The boswellic acids in frankincense creams have been shown to have pain-relieving and analgesic properties, helping to reduce discomfort and numbness in the hands.
remedy_parts: Roots: Frankincense roots contain a high concentration of boswellic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective for reducing numbness and pain in hands.

Barks: The barks of the frankincense plant contain oleo-gum resin, which is rich in terpenes and sesquiterpenes that help to reduce inflammation and promote healing, relieving numbness and discomfort in the hands.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality boswellia serrata resin and dry it thoroughly for at least 24 hours.
Grind the dried boswellia serrata resin into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Mix 100g of the ground boswellia serrata powder with 50ml of coconut oil in a double boiler setup for 30 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat and stir in 10 drops of sweet almond oil and 5 drops of lavender essential oil until well combined.
Pour the mixture into sterilized jars and allow it to cool and thicken at room temperature for at least 2 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric creams helps with numbness in hands because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and pain caused by conditions such as carpal tunnel syndrome. The cream's active compound, curcumin, also improves blood flow to the affected area, promoting oxygenation and nutrient delivery. Additionally, turmeric's natural antioxidant properties help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in nerve cells, reducing numbness and tingling sensations. By targeting the underlying causes of numbness, herbal turmeric creams provide effective relief for individuals experiencing hand numbness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Turmeric's active compound, curcumin, has potent anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and inflammation that may be contributing to numbness in the hands.
Antioxidant properties: Turmeric's antioxidants can neutralize free radicals that can damage nerve tissues and contribute to numbness, promoting healthy nerve function and reducing oxidative stress.

Pain-relieving properties: Curcumin has been shown to have analgesic and anti-nociceptive effects, which can help reduce pain associated with numbness in the hands, such as tingling, prickling, or burning sensations.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of turmeric contain a high concentration of curcuminoids, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help relieve numbness and tingling sensations in the hands.
Barks: Turmeric's barks are rich in flavonoids and phenolic compounds that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, making them effective in alleviating numbness and stiffness in the hands.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh turmeric root curcuma longa and wash it thoroughly under cold running water.
Dry the turmeric root in a warm place for 3 days or until it becomes brittle and breaks easily.
Grind 20g of dried turmeric powder into a fine paste using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 10g of beeswax and 5g of coconut oil with the turmeric paste in a double boiler setup for 30 minutes.
Remove from heat let cool to room temperature then whip until creamy and smooth taking about 10-15 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with numbness in hands because of its natural ability to improve blood circulation. The menthol in peppermint oil helps to dilate blood vessels, allowing more oxygen and nutrients to reach the extremities, which can become constricted due to poor circulation or nerve damage. As a result, the numbness and tingling sensations caused by poor circulation are alleviated, providing relief from discomfort and improving overall hand function.
remedy_properties: Pain-relieving menthol: Menthol's analgesic and anesthetic properties help reduce pain and numbness by blocking pain receptors and cooling the skin, providing temporary relief from tingling sensations.

Anti-inflammatory terpenes: Peppermint's terpene content, including limonene and eucalyptol, has anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the hands, which can contribute to numbness and discomfort.

Circulation-enhancing camphor: Camphor's vasodilatory properties help improve blood circulation to the hands, reducing numbness and tingling by increasing oxygen and nutrient delivery to affected areas.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in menthol and other compounds that help to stimulate blood flow and relieve numbness and tingling sensations in the hands.
Rhyzomes: Contain anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and discomfort associated with numbness, promoting healthy circulation and nerve function.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried peppermint leaves at least 2 weeks before use for maximum potency of menthol.
Blend 100g dried peppermint leaves with 200ml carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil and stir well.
Infuse the mixture in a warm dark place for 2-3 hours or overnight to allow for menthol extraction.
Strain the infused mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container discarding solids and filter residue.
Mix the extracted liquid with 10% beeswax and 1 tablespoon lanolin oil then whip until smooth and creamy taking approximately 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wintergreen creams helps with numbness in hands because they contain methyl salicylate, a natural analgesic that penetrates deep into the skin to target numbness caused by pressure on nerve endings. When applied topically, wintergreen creams stimulate blood flow and reduce inflammation, which can help alleviate numbness and tingling sensations in the hands. Additionally, the menthol present in wintergreen creams has a cooling effect that helps increase oxygenation of the tissues, further contributing to improved circulation and relief from numbness.
remedy_properties: Menthol: Menthol, a key active compound in wintergreen oil, helps to relieve numbness by stimulating nerve endings and increasing blood flow to the affected areas.

Cannabidiol (CBD): CBD, another prominent component of wintergreen oil, has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and discomfort associated with numbness, allowing for improved sensation and mobility.

Gingerols: Gingerols, a group of bioactive compounds found in wintergreen leaves, have natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects that help to soothe pain and numbness by reducing prostaglandins (hormone-like substances involved in pain transmission).
remedy_parts: Roots: Wintergreen roots contain menthol, which has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help relieve numbness and pain in the hands.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzome of wintergreen contains a high concentration of methyl salicylate, a natural pain reliever that is often used to treat numbness and tingling sensations in the hands.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh gaultheria procumbens leaves and stems of high-quality, organic source ensuring proper identification and handling procedures.
Dry the harvested plant material using a low-temperature oven at 40°c for 2 hours or air-drying method for 3-5 days to maintain potency.
Grind dried gaultheria procumbens into fine powder using mortar and pestle, extracting essential oils with carrier oil like coconut or sweet almond oil at ratio of 1:3.
Combine the extracted oil mixture with beeswax and shea butter in a double boiler, heating at 60-70°c for 15 minutes to create a smooth, creamy consistency.
Pour the final cream mixture into sterilized containers, allowing it to cool and thicken before use, storing in refrigeration for up to 6 months while maintaining shelf-life and potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum creams helps with numbness in hands because they contain a unique blend of herbal extracts that work to stimulate blood flow and reduce inflammation. The cream's active ingredients, such as Eucalyptus globulus and Melaleuca alternifolia, have natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help to relieve pressure on nerve endings, reducing numbness and tingling sensations. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Eucalyptus globulus contains compounds like eucalyptol and globulol that have anti-inflammatory effects, which may help reduce swelling and relieve pressure on nerves, potentially alleviating numbness.
Antispasmodic properties: The herb's active components, such as cineole and bornyl acetate, can help relax muscle spasms and ease cramping, which may contribute to numbness in the hands.

Analgesic and anesthetic properties: Eucalyptus globulus has been shown to have mild analgesic and anesthetic effects, which could potentially relieve pain and discomfort associated with numbness in the hands.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Tasmanian Blue Gum contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and pain associated with numbness in hands.
Barks: The barks of the Tasmanian Blue Gum contain tannins and flavonoids that help to reduce inflammation, improve blood circulation, and alleviate numbness and tingling sensations in the hands.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20 grams of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered eucalyptus globulus with 100 grams of cold-pressed coconut oil and 50 grams of beeswax in a double boiler setup.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture reaches a temperature of 160°f to 180°f.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to around 100°f to 120°f before whipping it with an electric mixer for 5 to 10 minutes or until it thickens into a creamy consistency.
Pour the cream into sterilized glass jars and seal them tightly, storing them in the refrigerator for up to 6 months or at room temperature for up to 3 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with numbness in hands because they contain the essential oil of Lavandula angustifolia, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties. This oil works to reduce swelling and ease pressure on nerve endings, thus alleviating numbness caused by conditions such as carpal tunnel syndrome, poor circulation, or repetitive strain injuries. As the cream is absorbed into the skin, it also helps to promote blood flow and relaxation, further reducing sensations of numbness and tingling in the hands.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Lavender's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and relieve pressure on nerves, which can contribute to numbness in the hands.
Analgesic: The analgesic properties of lavender cream provide pain relief, allowing individuals to regain feeling and sensation in their hands.
Antispasmodic: Lavender's antispasmodic properties help relax muscles and reduce muscle spasms, which can also contribute to numbness and tingling sensations in the hands.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help soothe numbness and pain in hands.
Buds: Containing a high concentration of essential oils, especially linalool and linalyl acetate, which provide analgesic and antispasmodic properties to relieve numbness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried lavender flowers of lavandula angustifolia in sufficient quantities for the recipe.
Dry the harvested lavender flowers by spreading them out in a single layer on paper bags for 7-10 days.
Mix one cup of dried lavender flowers with one cup of sweet almond oil in a double boiler setup and heat gently over low flame.
Infuse the mixture for 2 hours, then strain it through cheesecloth into another container and discard the solids.
Whip 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of coconut oil with the infused lavender oil until it reaches a creamy consistency suitable for application on the hands.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian creams helps with numbness in hands because they contain valerenic acids, which have a profound impact on the nervous system. By stimulating the production of GABA, a neurotransmitter that promotes relaxation and reduces anxiety, herbal valerian creams can help to calm overactive nerves that may be contributing to numbness or tingling sensations. remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in treating numbness and pain in hands.

remedy_recipe: Harvest dried valerian root powder from a reputable source for its calming effects on nervous system symptoms.
Mix the valerian root powder with sweet almond oil and beeswax in a double boiler for 30 minutes at medium heat.
Add vitamin e oil and lavender essential oil to the mixture and stir well to combine dosages of 10% valerian powder, 20% sweet almond oil, and 1% lavender oil.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool slightly before whipping into a creamy consistency for about 10 minutes.
Pour the cream into glass jars and store in a cool dark place for up to 6 months or refrigerate for up to 2 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams helps with numbness in hands because they increase blood circulation and reduce inflammation, which can alleviate tingling sensations. The anti-inflammatory compounds present in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, help to relax constricted blood vessels, allowing for improved oxygenation and nutrient supply to the extremities. remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of fresh ginger roots and wash them thoroughly under running water for 10 minutes.
Dry the harvested ginger roots in shade for 3 days or until they become brittle to improve quality.
Grind 15g of dried ginger into fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Combine 200ml of sweet almond oil, 100g of beeswax, and 30g of powdered ginger in a double boiler and heat gently for 1 hour.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass jar and let it cool before sealing for 24 hours to allow thickening.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme creams helps with numbness in hands because it contains natural anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm irritated nerves, reducing tingling and numbness sensations. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thyme leaves and dry them in a warm place for at least two weeks or store them in an airtight container.
Grind 20 grams of dried thyme leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for about five minutes.
Combine the thyme powder with 100 grams of sweet almond oil and heat the mixture gently over low heat for one hour.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it infuse for four hours before straining the cream through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container.
Add 20 milliliters of beeswax to the infused cream and stir well until melted then pour into sterilized jars and store in a cool place for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass creams helps with numbness in hands because of its ability to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation. remedy_recipe: Harvest cymbopogon citratus leaves at dawn when they are most potent and fresh for optimal extract quality.

Combine 10% by weight of the cymbopogon citratus essential oil with 90% by weight of coconut oil or sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 70°c for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally to ensure complete emulsification and homogenization.
Cool the cream to room temperature before transferring it to sterilized containers and storing it in the refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Saw palmetto creams helps with numbness in hands because they contain extracts that promote healthy hormone balance, particularly testosterone levels. remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried serenoa repens leaves, coconut oil, beeswax, and vitamin e oil.
Weigh 20 grams of dried serenoa repens leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Melt 100 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat for 30 minutes to create a warm carrier oil.
Add the ground serenoa repens powder to the melted coconut oil, stirring well to combine, then remove from heat and let steep for 1 hour.
Stir in 20 grams of beeswax and 10 milliliters of vitamin e oil into the cooled oil mixture until fully incorporated and a smooth cream forms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Balm-of-gilead creams helps with numbness in hands because they contain a unique blend of herbal extracts that stimulate blood circulation and relieve pressure on nerves. remedy_recipe: Harvest populus balsamifera bark, drying it for 24 hours before grinding into a fine powder weighing 30 grams.
Combine the powdered bark with 10 milliliters of olive oil and 5 milliliters of coconut oil, heating the mixture at low temperature for 3 hours.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth and discard the solids, transferring the liquid to a double boiler with 20 grams of beeswax and 15 grams of shea butter.
Heat the mixture over simmering water for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the wax and butter are fully melted and the mixture reaches a temperature of 60 degrees celsius.

Barks: Contain flavonoids and terpenoids that have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing numbness and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried calendula officinalis flowers and ensure they are free of contaminants.
Combine 20g of the dried flowers with 500ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil.
Steep the mixture in a glass container for 2-3 weeks, shaking daily to ensure proper extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids, reserving the infused oil.
Mix 1/4 cup of beeswax with 6 tablespoons of the infused calendula oil and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil, heating gently until melted.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with numbness in hands because they contain natural compounds that stimulate blood flow and improve circulation. The analgesic properties of fennel help to reduce inflammation and ease pressure on nerves, allowing for a reduction in numbness and tingling sensations. Additionally, the antispasmodic effects of fennel can help to relax muscle tension, which is often a contributing factor to numbness in the hands. Overall, herbal fennel creams provide a natural and effective way to alleviate numbness in the hands and promote overall hand health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Herbal fennel creams can help reduce inflammation in the affected areas, which can contribute to numbness in hands by reducing swelling and pressure on nerves.

Anxiolytic (stress-relieving) property: The calming effects of herbal fennel creams can help alleviate stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for numbness in hands, and promote relaxation and reduced muscle tension.

Vasoactive property: Herbal fennel creams contain compounds that can improve blood circulation, which is essential for maintaining healthy nerve function and preventing numbness and tingling sensations in the hands.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in reducing pain and discomfort associated with numbness in hands.
Barks: Fennel barks contain flavonoids and phenolic compounds that help reduce inflammation and promote blood flow to the affected areas, alleviating numbness and tingling sensations.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves measuring 2 ounces or 57 grams for the recipe.
Dry the harvested foeniculum vulgare leaves by spreading them out in a single layer for 7-10 days.
Grind the dried foeniculum vulgare leaves into a fine powder using an electric spice grinder for 1 minute.
Combine the ground foeniculum vulgare powder with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil and 1 tablespoon of beeswax in a small saucepan for 5 minutes.
Heat the mixture over low heat stirring occasionally until melted and smooth then remove from heat.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage creams helps with numbness in hands because they contain salvinorin A, a bioactive compound that has been shown to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation. The increased blood flow allows for more oxygen and nutrients to reach the affected areas, helping to alleviate tingling and numbness. Additionally, sage's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and discomfort, providing relief from symptoms associated with conditions such as carpal tunnel syndrome. As a result, herbal sage creams can be an effective natural remedy for treating numbness in hands.
remedy_properties: Thymol: Thymol, a compound found in sage, has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce pain and discomfort caused by numbness in hands.
Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid, a flavonoid found in sage, has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to protect nerve cells from damage and promote healthy blood flow to the hands.
Camphor: Camphor, a terpene found in sage, has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce pain and swelling caused by numbness in the hands.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the sage plant contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce numbness and pain in the hands.
Barks: Sage barks have been traditionally used to relieve numbness and tingling sensations in the hands due to their analgesic and antispasmodic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality salvia officinalis leaves and dry them properly for at least two weeks to preserve potency.
Grind dried salvia officinalis leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle, yielding approximately 20 grams of powder.
Combine the powder with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed coconut oil in a heat-resistant glass bowl, stirring well to ensure uniform distribution.
Heat the mixture gently over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it reaches a temperature of 60 degrees celsius to facilitate infusion.
Allow the mixture to cool and mature at room temperature for four weeks before transferring it to glass jars for storage and use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Field horsetail creams helps with numbness in hands because they contain bioactive compounds that stimulate blood flow and reduce inflammation. The cream's active ingredients, such as silica and flavonoids, also nourish and regenerate nerve endings, promoting healthy communication between nerve cells. As a result, herbal field horsetail creams can effectively alleviate numbness and tingling sensations in the hands, providing relief from conditions like carpal tunnel syndrome and peripheral neuropathy. This natural treatment option offers an effective alternative to conventional remedies for hand numbness.
remedy_properties: Silica content: Horsetail cream's high silica content helps to improve blood circulation, which can reduce numbness and tingling sensations in the hands by increasing oxygen and nutrient delivery to the affected areas.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in horsetail creams help to neutralize free radicals that can damage nerve tissues and contribute to numbness, promoting healthy nerve function and reducing inflammation.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Horsetail cream's anti-inflammatory properties can help to reduce swelling and pain associated with numbness, improving nerve conduction and reducing the risk of permanent nerve damage.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in silica, which helps stimulate blood flow and reduce inflammation, making it effective in treating numbness and tingling in the hands.
Rhyzomes: Containing high amounts of alkaloids, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to relieve numbness and pain in the hands.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh equisetum arvense leaves and dry them in a well-ventilated area for 2 weeks or use dried commercial leaves weighing 50g.
Grind the dried equisetum arvense leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle taking about 10 minutes.
Mix the powdered equisetum arvense with 100ml of cold-pressed coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat for 30 minutes.
Add 20g of beeswax to the mixture and stir until melted and fully incorporated into the blend taking approximately 15 minutes.
Remove the cream from the heat and let it cool and solidify at room temperature or place it in the refrigerator for about an hour before transferring it to airtight containers.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for numbness in hands are natural remedies that combine plant extracts and essential oils to provide relief from numbness, tingling, and discomfort in the hands. These creams work by improving blood circulation, reducing inflammation, and stimulating nerve function, which helps to alleviate numbness and restore sensation. Examples of herbal creams that help with numbness in hands include capsaicin-based creams, arnica oil, and peppermint cream. By using these creams, individuals can experience improved hand dexterity, reduced pain, and increased overall quality of life, allowing them to perform daily tasks with ease and confidence.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat numbness in hands with herbal creams is by applying topical creams containing natural ingredients such as Arnica, St. John's Wort, and Ginkgo Biloba. These herbs improve blood flow, reduce inflammation, and stimulate nerve function, helping to alleviate numbness. Apply the cream topically to the affected areas multiple times a day for best results. Consistency is key in treating numbness with herbal creams.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to numbness in hands that are treated with herbal creams are Raynaud's disease, arthritis, and fibromyalgia. These conditions cause a reduction in blood flow to the extremities, leading to feelings of numbness or tingling. Herbal creams containing ingredients such as arnica, capsaicin, and menthol can help increase blood flow, reduce pain and inflammation, and promote healing.
